message:
feat(algorithms): add multi-strategy greedy graph coloring

Extends greedy_color() with a new greedy_color_with_strategy() function
that supports four node ordering strategies for graph coloring, matching
NetworkX's greedy_color(strategy=...) API:

- "largest_first": processes nodes in descending degree order, breaking
  ties lexicographically. This is the new default (via greedy_color()
  which delegates to this strategy), matching NetworkX's default.

- "smallest_last": iteratively removes the smallest-degree node from
  the remaining graph, then reverses the removal order. This tends to
  produce good colorings by ensuring high-degree nodes are colored last
  when fewer constraints remain.

- "random_sequential": uses insertion order with no sorting. Useful as
  a baseline or when deterministic ordering isn't needed.

- "DSATUR" / "saturation_largest_first": dynamic saturation ordering
  that picks the uncolored node with the highest saturation (most
  distinct colors among its already-colored neighbors), breaking ties
  by degree then lexicographic order. Colors nodes during the ordering
  pass itself since saturation depends on coloring state. This often
  produces near-optimal colorings for sparse graphs.

- Any unrecognized strategy falls back to lexicographic (canonical)
  order for backwards compatibility.

The existing greedy_color() function now delegates to
greedy_color_with_strategy(graph, "largest_first") so all existing
callers get the improved default without code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
